title: Make ts2hx fail closed on unsupported source
description: ts2hx can silently omit whole source files or unsupported top-level statements and still exit successfully. Introduce structured translation outcomes so every input file and top-level statement is either emitted or diagnosed, with strict mode returning nonzero and no behavior-changing placeholder presented as success.
design: First land the failure contract around the existing emitter: structured diagnostics with source spans, explicit strict versus assisted policy, deterministic exit codes, and transactional output. Preserve existing supported snapshots. A semantic IR remains follow-up work rather than being invented inside this fix.
acceptance_criteria: No source file is skipped through if-not-emitted continue without a diagnostic; unsupported top-level statements fail strict mode; output is not partially committed on strict failure; diagnostics are deterministic and machine-readable; ts2hx tests and root yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: ts2hx now gives every root file an explicit disposition, diagnoses every unsupported top-level statement, removes unsupported throw placeholders, preserves prior output on strict failure, writes deterministic assisted manifests/markers transactionally, and exposes CLI exit codes 0/1/2/3. yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test and full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Restore precise classic Null declarations
description: Classic Genes declaration emission maps Haxe Null<T> to any, regressing vanilla null unions and weakening strict consumers. Restore precise nullable unions and add a strict external consumer gate so runtime-only tests cannot mask declaration regressions.
design: Reuse the existing TypeEmitter null-union logic in both TypeScript and classic declaration profiles while preserving the genes.ts.no_null_union override only where explicitly supported. Add a generated classic declaration consumer with skipLibCheck false and an expected rejection for assigning Map.get directly to a non-null value.
acceptance_criteria: Classic haxe.Constraints.IMap.get emits V | null or null | V rather than any; strict consumer accepts nullable use and rejects non-null assignment; npm test, the declaration consumer, and yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Classic Null<T> declarations now emit nullable unions and a strict NodeNext external consumer passes; yarn test:ci passed.

title: Emit complete closed TypeScript interfaces
description: Generated TypeScript interfaces currently receive an unconditional string index signature returning any. That masks DCE-trimmed public members and arbitrary consumer typos. Capture declared public interface fields before generator/DCE mutation, emit the complete closed surface, and reserve index signatures for explicit indexable source constructs.
design: Introduce a narrow public-interface surface cache at the existing onAfterTyping seam and consume it in TsModuleEmitter. Do not add a downstream special case or a general compiler rewrite. Add a TypeScript consumer that requires the full IMap surface and proves an unknown member is rejected.
acceptance_criteria: Generated haxe.Constraints.IMap includes get, set, exists, remove, keys, iterator, keyValueIterator, copy, toString, and clear; ordinary interfaces emit no catch-all index signature; a @ts-expect-error consumer proves unknown members are rejected; focused genes-ts full tests and yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Complete pre-DCE public interface capture now emits closed, full IMap surfaces; negative TypeScript consumer rejects unknown members; yarn test:ci passed.

title: genes-ts: normalize Rest casts from Reflect.fields
description: Discovered from A downstream config loader. Using Haxe std Reflect.fields can generate Register.unsafeCast<Rest<any>>(...) in Reflect.ts without importing Rest, breaking TS strict typecheck. Normalize Rest cast targets to array types or otherwise avoid leaking unresolved Rest in expression casts.
acceptance_criteria: A genes-ts fixture using Reflect.fields typechecks; generated Reflect.ts does not contain unsafeCast<Rest<...>> or unresolved Rest; genes-ts tests pass.
close_reason: accepted: Reflect.fields fixture passes; TypeEmitter normalizes haxe.extern.Rest<T> TType aliases to T[]; full test guard rejects unsafeCast<Rest<...>> leaks; gates passed: yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts:full, reran yarn test:genes-ts && yarn test:genes-ts:full after guard.

title: CI: fix classic matrix + unblock mac runner
description: Classic Genes job currently runs only on macos-latest (matrix misconfigured) and fails, blocking CI. Rework matrix to explicitly include ubuntu+node20/22 and macos+node20 (allow-failure) so CI stays green while we debug mac.
acceptance_criteria: - Classic Genes runs on ubuntu (Node 20 + 22)\n- macos-latest run is marked allow-failure (non-blocking)\n- genes-ts CI run is green on main
close_reason: Fix classic job matrix; make macos non-blocking until resolved.

title: CI: fix invalid workflow using env context in job name
description: GitHub Actions refuses to parse ci.yml because job.name uses an expression with env context. Replace with matrix.node-version (allowed in job name) and keep behavior unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: - GitHub Actions no longer reports invalid workflow\n- Workflow shows proper name (genes-ts CI) instead of file path\n- Release workflow_run still points to genes-ts CI
close_reason: Use matrix.node-version in job name; remove env usage that breaks Actions parsing.

title: Extract shared PublicSurface model
description: Replace target-local public member collection with an immutable PublicSurface model for exported classes, interfaces, typedefs, inheritance, overload identity, visibility, and type-parameter substitution. Both TS implementation interfaces and classic declaration emission consume it.
design: Build on the landed pre-DCE closed-interface fix. First compare the model against existing TS and d.ts surfaces, retain runtime-marker emission as a separate concern, and migrate consumers without reopening interfaces or coupling collection to printing.
acceptance_criteria: TS interfaces and classic declarations consume the same checked public member facts; inherited/generic/overloaded members have focused fixtures; implementation-private and runtime-only fields do not leak; current negative consumers and yarn test:ci pass.
close_reason: Landed the shared pre-DCE PublicSurface model and migrated TypeScript interfaces plus classic declarations to it, including inherited generic members, overload identity, visibility boundaries, and paired negative/runtime fixtures. Full yarn test:ci passes.

title: Add exact source maps determinism and output budgets
description: Upgrade source-map and output-quality evidence with exact token mappings, mapped thrown stacks, clean-build tree hashes, and per-fixture byte/token/temp/module/import metrics.
design: Compare semantics rather than byte identity with vanilla. Begin performance as report-only until noise is calibrated. Require reviewed manifest entries for legitimate size/temp/module growth.
acceptance_criteria: Two clean builds hash identically after documented normalization; representative TS/classic tokens and stack frames map to exact Haxe locations; unexplained module growth or no-temp fixture regressions fail; budgets are checked in and deterministic.

title: Introduce target-neutral JSX intent and capability policy
description: Represent tags/components/props/children as target-neutral JSX intent before printing and make TSX, TypeScript createElement, and classic JS capabilities explicit.
design: Preserve inline markup as default-on in genes.ts mode and marker compatibility during migration. Classic mode must either lower through a configured runtime or fail before output with a source-positioned capability diagnostic.
acceptance_criteria: Existing TSX profiles stay green; invalid intrinsic/component prop and child fixtures are rejected; same-source classic JSX either executes equivalent intent or receives a stable pre-output diagnostic; docs match behavior; yarn test:ci passes.
close_reason: Implemented shared target-neutral JsxPlan and capability policy consumed by TypeScript and classic JS emitters; moved JSX runtime imports into DependencyPlan; added fail-closed source-positioned diagnostics, exact-once lifted-value semantics, TS negative cases, and identical-source TSX/classic runtime fixtures. Updated contracts/docs and deterministic todoapp snapshots. Verified with yarn test:genes-ts:tsx, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:dual-output, and full yarn test:ci (306 classic assertions, TS 5.5/6/7, Playwright 3/3, ts2hx 53 snapshots + 3 roundtrips + strict diagnostics) in 192.03s.

title: Centralize TS5 TS6 TS7 and Haxe toolchain lanes
description: Centralize compiler versions and distinguish generated-code compatibility from programmatic TypeScript API compatibility. Keep Haxe 4.3.7 blocking, add TS6 API and TS7 generated-output lanes, and track Haxe 5 preview nonblocking.
design: Use one checked-in version manifest consumed by scripts and CI. Do not pretend TS7 exposes the historical Program API; isolate ts2hx/dts2hx API use behind an adapter. Remove obsolete config assumptions through fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: No repeated hard-coded TypeScript version literals in test runners; required generated projects check under the documented TS lanes; TS API tools use the supported bridge lane; Haxe preview is visible but nonblocking; version docs match CI.
close_reason: Centralized Node/Haxe/TypeScript versions in config/toolchains.json; added TS5 generated-output floor, TS6 Program/TypeChecker bridge and output lane, TS7 output-only lane, blocking stable Haxe/Node lanes, and visible nonblocking Haxe 5 preview CI. Replaced runner literals with manifest-backed commands, isolated compiler API imports behind adapters, removed obsolete baseUrl/node10 assumptions, added cross-version ArrayBuffer and semantic-audit compatibility fixes, documented lane ownership, validated workflows with actionlint, and passed full yarn test:ci in 216.38s.

title: Extract runtime type and declaration DependencyPlan
description: Replace no-op type printing and conservative reachability workarounds with an immutable dependency plan whose edges distinguish runtime values, runtime side effects, type-only symbols, and declaration-only symbols.
design: Retain the existing Dependencies import-form machinery. First run graph extraction in shadow/compare mode, add provenance and unresolved diagnostics, then switch emitters after output and DCE invariants are proven.
acceptance_criteria: No-op printer discovery and silent Context.getType catch are removed; DCE/type-only fixtures pass; every referenced symbol has a validated edge/global; runtime imports/module counts stay within reviewed baselines; yarn test:ci passes.
notes:
PublicSurface integration established a deliberate temporary boundary: classic class declarations intersect the pre-DCE public surface with post-DCE runtime fields, while interfaces remain complete. Removing that intersection requires declaration-only edges plus explicit abstract-instance surface modeling; blindly emitting every KAbstractImpl field can expose invalid static owner generics (for example an instance Future<T>.status represented on the static implementation class). The final plan must retain referenced declaration types without broadening classic JS DCE.
Landing correction: declaration-only reachability is now independent, but the default classic class declaration intentionally remains intersected with post-DCE runtime members. Removing that boundary would make .d.ts promise missing JavaScript. Complete class-library surfaces now belong to the separate opt-in reusable-library profile created from this issue. Shadow comparison matched the full TS and classic import trees; full yarn test:ci passed in 191.81s.
close_reason: Landed immutable runtime/type/declaration DependencyPlan edges with typed refs and provenance; removed sink-printer discovery and silent Context.getType recovery; preserved import alias parity via ordered edges; separated implementation and declaration expansion so classic DCE stays compact; added TS type-only and classic declaration-only/no-orphan-map fixtures; shadow checks, focused gates, snapshots, and full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Extract shared NullishContract semantics
description: Represent Haxe Null<T>, JavaScript undefined, optional properties, Undefinable<T>, native map absence, and iterator completion as distinct shared semantic facts consumed by TS, classic JS, and declaration emission.
design: Introduce a narrow semantic classifier before expanding any target IR. Target printers choose syntax, but may not silently redefine absence semantics. Add paired TS/classic fixtures.
acceptance_criteria: A checked nullish matrix distinguishes missing, undefined, null, optional, and T under strict TS flags and classic runtime traces; no scattered Map-specific target check remains as the sole model; yarn test:ci passes.
close_reason: Landed shared NullishContract across TS, classic JS decisions, and declaration emission with exact strict consumers, paired runtime traces, precedence-safe projections, docs, and green full CI.

title: Add semantic exported-surface and negative type gates
description: Go beyond lexical any/unknown scanning by auditing generated exported TypeScript and classic declaration surfaces with the TypeScript Compiler API, AST index-signature policy, strict external consumers, and negative @ts-expect-error fixtures.
design: Keep the cheap scanner, but treat it only as one layer. Add provenance/owner/reason for allowed runtime boundaries. Build on the closed-interface and classic-nullability P0 fixes without reopening types.
acceptance_criteria: Compiler-API export audit detects explicit and inferred public any/unknown; ordinary index signatures are rejected unless explicitly modeled; negative consumers fail if invalid member/nullish/JSX operations become legal; yarn test:ci passes.
close_reason: Landed TypeScript-Compiler-API exported-surface auditing, exact provenance boundary manifests, unsafe/inferred/imported type and index-signature fixtures, TS/classic IMap enforcement, JSX negative-consumer guards, and a blocking CI script. Full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Architecture and product-readiness roadmap
description: Implement the evidence-backed post-audit roadmap for genes-ts as a shared Haxe-to-TypeScript and classic Haxe-to-JavaScript compiler. Preserve classic runtime behavior, strengthen TypeScript/public declarations, and borrow only narrow architecture/testing concepts from sibling compilers.
design: Proceed incrementally through shared semantic facts and lightweight normalized nodes: PublicSurface, NullishContract, DependencyPlan, TempPlan/NamePlan, and JsxIntent. Do not port to Reflaxe or build separate TS/classic semantic engines. Each child lands with generic fixtures and full yarn test:ci.
acceptance_criteria: All P1 child gates pass; both output modes remain first-class; remaining P2 work is represented by explicit Beads and generated evidence never overstates scope.

title: ts2hx React type-only imports must emit compileable typed Haxe
description: A generic TSX fixture with type-only imports from react currently reports success but emits unresolved ReactElement/MouseEvent/HTMLAnchorElement names plus a generated React extern containing Dynamic. It also maps TS optional/undefined JSX props to null, weakening React prop semantics. Reduce the constructs into a reusable fixture and make ts2hx output compileable, typed Haxe without Dynamic/untyped or downstream-specific rules.
acceptance_criteria: A generic React TSX fixture exercises type-only ReactElement and generic MouseEvent<HTMLAnchorElement>, dynamic intrinsic tags, optional JSX attributes/handlers, fragments, map callbacks, and nullable children; generated Haxe has no Dynamic/untyped/unsupported placeholders, compiles through genes-ts, re-emits strict TSX, and focused plus full genes CI pass.
close_reason: Both callable-guard operand orders are generically covered and full CI passes

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Implemented generic React/TSX migration support: typed ReactElement/MouseEvent type-only imports, DOM element mapping, TS optional-field preservation, undefined JSX values, JS truthiness and callable guards, indexed Array callbacks, dynamic intrinsic React tags, nullable JSX child typing, and lifted JSX marker prop recovery in genes-ts. Added react-types fixture with strong-Haxe guard, Haxe compile smoke, strict genes-ts TSX roundtrip, and no-any/unknown user-module guard. Passed focused yarn --cwd tools/ts2hx test and full yarn test:ci (208.18s, including classic, genes-ts acceptance, 3 todoapp browser E2E tests, security, versions, and ts2hx).

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa

comments.author: Marcelo Serpa
comments.text: Follow-up symmetric callable guard lowering is green: the generic fixture now covers both typeof callback === "function" and "function" === typeof callback. WPHX-404's strong-Haxe gate passes generated-Haxe compile, strict TSX roundtrip, and zero weak user-module markers. Full yarn test:ci passed in 122.21s, including security/version checks, 288 classic assertions, acceptance, 3 Playwright E2E tests, 48 ts2hx snapshot files, and 3 roundtrip fixtures.

title: ts2hx: preserve module-local bindings and regex replacements
description: WPHX-403's real TypeScript leaf-package roundtrip reduced three generic ts2hx gaps: calls to non-exported module functions are emitted without a Haxe-visible owner, references to module-level RegExp constants are not lowered as value references, and String.replace with RegExp/string arguments falls back to an unsupported throw. Add minimal compiler fixtures and generic lowering so these ordinary TypeScript constructs roundtrip through Haxe and genes-ts without downstream names or patches.
acceptance_criteria: Minimal generic fixtures cover private module helper calls, module-level RegExp constants, and RegExp string replacement; generated Haxe compiles and runs through the roundtrip harness; full yarn test:ci passes.
notes: Reduced from WPHX-403. Generic fix preserves module-private declarations, maps TypeScript RegExp to typed Haxe EReg, and lowers regex test/replace via checker-aware EReg calls. Added module-regexp snapshot and full TS→Haxe→genes-ts→strict TS runtime roundtrip. yarn test:ci passed on 2026-07-09/10.
close_reason: Generic module-local binding and RegExp roundtrip support implemented with minimized fixture; full yarn test:ci passed.

title: Fix high-severity sigstore vulnerability blocking full CI
description: The mandatory yarn test:ci gate fails in test:vulns because osv-scanner reports GHSA-52v5-jr5w-gjxr against sigstore@3.1.0. Resolve the dependency generically in genes-ts without downstream-specific changes, preserve lockfile determinism, and rerun the complete compiler CI gate. Discovered by WordPressHX WPHX-401 at commit 85d26cf4e7b853297a99aa26cf3651e187f45b7a.
acceptance_criteria: yarn test:vulns reports no unallowlisted high/critical vulnerabilities; yarn test:ci passes classic Genes JS, genes-ts TypeScript, acceptance, snapshots/runtime, security, version, and ts2hx gates; package and lockfile changes are committed and pushed.
close_reason: Resolved GHSA-52v5-jr5w-gjxr by forcing sigstore 4.1.1 in the generic dependency resolution and refreshing yarn.lock. yarn test:vulns reports no issues and the full yarn test:ci gate passes secrets, vulnerabilities, versions, classic Genes JS runtime tests, genes-ts TypeScript acceptance/snapshots/runtime, todoapp Playwright, and ts2hx snapshots/roundtrips.

title: genes-ts: metadata-gate private helper lowering
description: OpenCodeHX build coverage showed global private static lowering is still too broad for existing downstream code. Keep the generic lowering implementation, but require an explicit metadata marker on private static helpers so projects can clean declaration surfaces at known helper boundaries without changing unrelated class methods.
acceptance_criteria:
- Private static helper lowering requires an explicit metadata marker such as @:genesLowerPrivateHelper
- The basic genes-ts fixture proves annotated sync and async static helpers lower out of declarations
- The no-js-es polish fixture no longer opts into helper lowering
- yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:ci, and OpenCodeHX npm run build pass
close_reason: accepted: private static helper lowering now requires explicit @:genesLowerPrivateHelper metadata; basic fixture covers sync and async annotated helpers; no-js-es no longer opts in; yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:ci, and OpenCodeHX npm run build pass

title: genes-ts: scope private helper lowering to static methods
description: OpenCodeHX build coverage showed the new private helper lowering is too broad for instance methods: lowered private instance/accessor/generic helpers can lose prototype availability, class type parameters, or method-value paths. Scope the feature to private static source helpers for the original d.ts leak, preserve async helper emission, and leave instance lowering for a later dedicated fixture.
acceptance_criteria:
- -D genes.ts.lower_private_helpers lowers private static source helper methods out of class declarations
- Private instance methods remain class methods under the define
- Async private static helpers emit async module helper functions
- yarn test:genes-ts and OpenCodeHX npm run build pass
close_reason: accepted: scoped private helper lowering to private static source methods, preserved async helper emission, kept private instance methods class-shaped, and proved with yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, and yarn test:ci

title: genes-ts: hide private helper methods from generated declarations
description: Private Haxe helper methods in user classes are emitted as public TypeScript class methods in genes-ts source output. When downstream projects run tsc declaration emit, those helpers become part of the public .d.ts surface even though they are implementation details. Reduce this as a generic genes-ts class emission issue, not a downstream-specific case.
acceptance_criteria:
- Private source-owned Haxe helper methods used by public methods do not appear by name in tsc-emitted .d.ts files
- Generated TS still typechecks and runs when helpers are called directly or passed as function values
- Haxe stdlib/js support helper methods keep their existing runtime-accessible shape
- yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, and yarn test:ci pass
close_reason: accepted: private source helper methods can lower to unexported module helpers behind -D genes.ts.lower_private_helpers; fixture checks prove helper names stay out of tsc declarations; yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, and yarn test:ci pass

title: Add generic genes.ts JSON value and codec helpers
description: Downstream OpenCodeHX exposed the need for a reusable target-polymorphic native JSON value layer: recursive TypeScript JsonValue/JsonObject/JsonArray aliases, checked Json.value/object/array construction for Haxe-owned literals/records, JsonCodec.parse/stringify/narrow runtime validation for Unknown boundaries, and paired genes-ts/classic Genes fixtures. This must be generic compiler/library work with no OpenCodeHX product knowledge.
acceptance_criteria: genes.ts exposes generic JSON value/object/array primitives with recursive TS aliases; Haxe-owned JSON literals/records compile without Unknown.fromBoundary, any, or call-site casts; Unknown boundaries narrow through runtime JsonCodec guards; negative fixtures reject non-JSON values; classic Genes output preserves native JS runtime shapes; full genes CI gate passes.
close_reason: accepted: added generic genes.ts JsonValue/JsonObject/JsonArray/JsonPrimitive/JsonNonNullValue primitives, checked Json.value/object/array constructors, JsonCodec parse/narrow/stringify with documented unsafeAssumeJson containment, recursive TS alias emission including downstream abstracts over JsonValue, array/rest union-parenthesis emission, runtime JsonValue tests, and json-abstract snapshot fixture; also resolved the existing @sigstore/core OSV lockfile gate with a resolution so full CI is clean. Gates: yarn test:genes-ts:minimal, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test, yarn test:vulns, yarn test:versions, SKIP_CLASSIC=1 yarn test:acceptance, yarn test:ts2hx, yarn test:ci, git diff --check.

title: Fix generic @:async method type parameters
description: @:async build macro failed to resolve method-local type parameters in Promise<T> return annotations, causing generic async helpers to fail with Type not found : T. Fix must preserve the declared generic signature while keeping Promise-shape inspection typed and local.
acceptance_criteria: Aggregate CI result is recorded, with unrelated blockers tracked separately.
notes: Implemented macro-only local type-parameter erasure for Promise shape inspection so @:async methods can keep Promise<T> return signatures. Added TestAsyncAwait.genericIdentity<T> regression fixture. Validation run: yarn test, yarn test:genes-ts:minimal, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots (rerun standalone after parallel artifact race), yarn test:genes-ts:sourcemaps, yarn test:genes-ts:tsx, yarn test:versions, yarn test:ts2hx, SKIP_CLASSIC=1 yarn test:acceptance. yarn test:ci is blocked by pre-existing OSV @sigstore/core finding tracked as genes-2wl.
close_reason: Fixed generic @:async Promise<T> method handling with macro-only type-param erasure and regression coverage; focused gates pass. Full yarn test:ci remains blocked by unrelated OSV issue genes-2wl.

title: Resolve OSV finding for @sigstore/core
description: Full yarn test:ci currently stops at yarn test:vulns because osv-scanner reports GHSA-jfc7-64v2-mr8c for @sigstore/core 2.0.0 in yarn.lock, fixed in 3.2.1. This appears unrelated to compiler async changes but blocks the mandatory aggregate CI script.
acceptance_criteria: yarn test:vulns passes without suppressing the finding, or the vulnerability is documented/allowlisted with an explicit maintainer-approved reason.
close_reason: Verified yarn test:vulns reports no issues with the resolved lockfile; full yarn test:ci passed.

title: genes-ts: add unknown narrowing primitives
description: Add reusable genes.ts primitives for Unknown narrowing, read-only unknown records, and read-only unknown arrays. The helpers must stay generic, work in TypeScript output and classic JS output, and keep domain/schema decoding outside the compiler.
close_reason: accepted: added generic UnknownNarrow/UnknownRecord/UnknownArray helpers, fixed raw undefined preservation in genes-ts placeholders, covered with snapshots, strict generated TS, classic runtime tests, and full CI.

title: genes-ts: parenthesize nullish operands in null comparisons
description: OpenCodeHX exposed generated TypeScript like options.metadata ?? null != null, which TypeScript parses as options.metadata ?? (null != null). genes-ts should preserve Haxe comparison semantics by parenthesizing nullish-coalescing operands when they are emitted inside == null or != null comparisons.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a generic genes-ts snapshot fixture for a nullish-coalescing operand compared with null
- Generated TypeScript emits (expr ?? null) != null rather than expr ?? null != null
- yarn test:ci passes
close_reason: accepted: fixed generic genes-ts null comparison emission for nullish-coalescing operands; added BoundaryTypes snapshot coverage; yarn test:ci passed

title: genes-ts: parenthesize raw syntax templates before chained access
description: A downstream Haxe expression of the generic shape value.orNull()[0].field exposed malformed TypeScript from genes.ts.Undefinable.orNull(), which lowers through js.Syntax.code('{0} ?? null', value). When that raw nullish-coalescing template is used as the receiver of array/field access, generated TS can become '(value ?? null) ?? null[0].field' instead of parenthesizing the raw expression before the chained access. This is a generic expression-precedence/codegen bug, not downstream-specific.
acceptance_criteria: Add a minimal genes-ts fixture using genes.ts.Undefinable<Array<T>>.orNull()[0].field or an equivalent js.Syntax.code nullish-coalescing receiver; generated TypeScript parenthesizes correctly and strict tsc passes; existing raw template/native-field behavior stays intact; yarn test:genes-ts, snapshots/full as relevant, classic tests, and full yarn test:ci pass before downstream work resumes.
close_reason: accepted: fixed raw js.Syntax.code placeholder-template receivers before chained access; added Undefinable<Array<T>>.orNull()[0].field snapshot fixture; verified yarn test:genes-ts, yarn test:genes-ts:full, yarn build:example:todoapp, git diff --check, and full yarn test:ci

title: genes-ts: import promise thenable casts in full output
description: Full Genes CI after restoring the vulnerability gate reaches test:acceptance but tsc fails in tests/genes-ts/full/out/src-gen/tests/TestAsyncAwait.ts: generated code emits Register.unsafeCast<ThenableStruct<any>>(null) without importing or declaring ThenableStruct in that module. The alias exists in generated js/lib/Promise.ts, so type dependency/import planning needs to cover this generic special type when it appears in emitted casts.
acceptance_criteria: Full Genes CI passes. A focused fixture or existing full fixture proves emitted ThenableStruct casts either import the alias, qualify it, or avoid the unresolved type; no downstream-specific codegen special cases are added.
close_reason: accepted: existing TestAsyncAwait full fixture now emits await Promise.resolve(null) without ThenableStruct<any>; yarn test:genes-ts:full and full yarn test:ci passed

title: genes-ts: preserve call argument context inside raw syntax placeholders
description: A downstream async HTTP client reduction exposed that @:await lowers through js.Syntax.code("await {0}", call), and raw syntax placeholder emission currently delegates the placeholder expression to the base JS value emitter. That bypasses genes-ts call-argument expected-type context, so optional anonymous fields passed to Null<T> parameters emit T | null | undefined inside awaited calls. Fix this generically while preserving raw template behavior and snapshot it with an async/await fixture.
close_reason: accepted: raw js.Syntax.code placeholders now emit placeholder expressions through the TS value emitter, preserving call-argument expected-type context inside @:await. Added AsyncFoo metadata-await fixture where an optional field is passed to a Null<String> parameter inside an awaited Promise call. Gates: yarn test:genes-ts; UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test; git diff --check. Full yarn test:ci remains blocked by pre-existing OSV dependency findings tracked as genes-6f5.

title: genes-ts: preserve TS field emission when normalizing optional reads
description: Follow-up from optional-field nullable-param work. The new emitValue optional-read normalization used the base JS emitter for the inner field, so nested reads like record.server.port emitted (record.server.port ?? null) and skipped the TS emitter's nullable-receiver assertion. Fix the helper generically so optional-read normalization reuses TS field emission and preserves strict-null output for nested optional fields.
close_reason: accepted: optional-field normalization now re-enters TS expression emission and suppresses nested optional-field null normalization only while emitting a receiver that is immediately guarded by a TS non-null assertion. Added nested optional-field fixture and snapshot. Gates: yarn test:genes-ts; UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test; git diff --check.

title: genes-ts: normalize optional fields when passed to Null parameters
description: Discovered from a downstream Copilot HTTP client slice. Haxe optional anonymous fields can be absent at runtime, and Haxe treats absent reads as null-like, but generated strict TypeScript passes T | null | undefined into helpers typed as Null<T> (T | null), causing TS2345. Reduce to a generic fixture where an optional field is passed directly to a Null<T> parameter and fix genes-ts without downstream-specific code.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal genes-ts fixture passes an optional anonymous field directly to a Null<T> parameter and strict TypeScript accepts the output; generated TS normalizes the optional read consistently with Haxe null semantics; existing genes-ts gates pass; the reduced downstream HTTP-client case typechecks without a source workaround.
close_reason: accepted: optional anonymous-field reads now normalize to null when emitted into Null<T> expected-value contexts, while genes.ts.Undefinable<T> remains an explicit undefined contract. Added BoundaryTypes fixture and snapshot for optional field passed directly to nullable parameter. Gates: yarn test:genes-ts, UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots, yarn test, git diff --check. Full yarn test:ci currently stops in pre-existing OSV dependency scan; tracked separately as genes-6f5.

title: genes-ts: preserve native field names in optional helper lowering

Reduce this to the native anonymous field fixture and fix the emitter generically.

- Add a minimal genes-ts fixture covering @:native anonymous field access through an Undefinable/optional helper

- Downstream consumer builds no longer fail on native-field helper lowering
close_reason: accepted: fixed generic genes-ts js.Syntax.code placeholder emission so template arguments use genes' raw JS value path, preserving @:native anonymous field names without injecting TS strict-cast machinery. Added BoundaryTypes snapshot coverage for native Undefinable field orNull lowering. Gates passed: yarn test:genes-ts; yarn test:genes-ts:snapshots; yarn test:genes-ts:full; yarn test; git diff --check.

This produces strict TypeScript errors with fields such as @:native("function") final fn. Fix generically with fixtures; do not add downstream-specific knowledge.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal genes-ts fixture covers @:native anonymous fields in Array<T>.push arguments and EitherType object arms; generated TypeScript emits the native property names and strict-checks; relevant genes-ts gates and classic yarn test pass.
close_reason: accepted: fixed generic object-literal context for typed call arguments and EitherType object arms. Fixtures cover @:native fields in Array<T>.push and EitherType return object arms. title: genes-ts: preserve Undefinable absent in assignments

Fix generically without downstream-specific knowledge.

Undefinable<T> should preserve undefined at expression/codegen boundaries without leaking null.
acceptance_criteria: A minimal genes-ts fixture assigns Undefinable.absent() inside an object/typedef field and strict TypeScript sees undefined, not null; generated TS contains undefined for absent values; relevant genes-ts snapshot/type gates pass.

Require semantic traces and output budgets before switching printers.

title: React HXX: type-safe TSX-like authoring in Haxe
description:
Add a React authoring DSL using an HXX-style macro so users can write TSX-like markup in Haxe and compile it with genes-ts.

Deliverables:
- `genes.react.JSX.jsx` (macro) that parses JSX-ish markup and generates React element expressions
- Output modes:
  - Default: emit `.tsx` and print TSX markup for React element expressions
  - Low-level: emit `.ts` and keep `React.createElement(...)` calls
- JSX runtime:
  - Default: TypeScript `jsx: "react-jsx"` (automatic runtime)
  - Opt-in classic runtime support
- Intrinsic elements are **always typed** (no Dynamic fallback), including `data-*` / `aria-*`
- Optional inline markup support (rewrite `@:markup` -> `JSX.jsx(...)`)
- Tests that compile under strict genes-ts harness and run with `react-dom/server`

Reference pattern: reflaxe.elixir HXX + InlineMarkup.
acceptance_criteria:
- React authoring macro: genes.react.JSX.jsx
- Optional inline markup rewrite: genes.react.InlineMarkup.enable() (gated by -D genes.react.inline_markup)
- TSX mode: emit .tsx + print TSX markup from JSX markers (default automatic runtime)
- Low-level mode: emit .ts + lower JSX markers into React.createElement calls
- Opt-in classic runtime: -D genes.ts.jsx_classic injects React import for TSX output
- Intrinsic elements typed per-tag incl. data-*/aria-*
- Tests: npm run test:genes-ts:tsx (strict + runtime via react-dom/server)
close_reason: Closed
